title: The rise of artificial intelligence drives massive investments in chips, data centers and energy storage
summary: Companies like Bloom Energy, EMCOR, Micron and Kyocera lead a wave of infrastructure spending to meet the growing demand for AI.

title: Global markets react to Warsh's evasiveness about interest rates and to weak US employment data
summary: The new Fed chair avoids making commitments regarding the July meeting, while the US private sector creates less employment than expected.

title: Fear grows of an artificial intelligence bubble while the 'Magnificent Seven' lose $2.3 trillion on the stock market
summary: Skepticism about the high valuations of big tech causes a sector rotation and doubts about the sustainability of the AI rally.

title: Bitcoin suffers its worst month in four years as it plummets 20% in June, while MiCA regulation comes into effect in the EU
summary: The leading cryptocurrency depreciates in a context of regulatory tightening and legal actions against Binance in London.

title: Wall Street closes its best half-year in six years, but uncertainty about rates and tech stocks marks the start of the third quarter
summary: US indices start July cautiously after a record first half, in a day marked by the Fed's speech and employment data.

title: European financial sector restructures with changes at the top of German savings banks and the rise of private credit
summary: German banking renews its leadership, PIK credit generates warnings, and giants like BlackRock and Invesco compete for revenue growth.

title: Can Fite soars 65% on its pancreatic cancer data; ABIVAX and Kyowa Kirin also move in the biopharmaceutical sector
summary: The sector experiences an intense day with positive clinical trial results, new stock offerings, and regulation of advanced therapies.

title: Meta plans to launch a cloud computing business to sell its excess artificial intelligence server capacity
summary: The company seeks to monetize its massive AI infrastructure by offering cloud services to other companies, which boosts its shares.

title: Eurozone inflation falls to 2.8% in June and the ECB studies doubling minimum reserves to ease pressure on banks
summary: The moderation of prices reinforces expectations of a turn in the monetary policy of the European Central Bank.

title: Geely and BYD show opposing trends in sales; Wayve and Lime innovate in autonomous and urban mobility
summary: The automotive and mobility sector is divided between the growth of Chinese exports and the challenges of the domestic market.

title: Alcoa and South32 close a $4.8 billion megadeal; Schroders and ACS lead the day's corporate operations
summary: The mergers and acquisitions market shows intense activity with transactions in the mining, financial and infrastructure sectors.

title: Trump reports income of over $1 billion on his return to the presidency, boosted by the bull market
summary: The president's financial disclosure reveals large gains in his businesses, attributing his success to the stock market rally.

title: Americans fear taxes will drastically cut their Social Security checks, but the actual calculation is less aggressive
summary: Confusion about the taxation of retirement benefits creates alarm, although the well-known '85% rule' is not always applied as feared.

title: Gold heads for its worst quarter in more than a decade as retail frenzy fades
summary: The precious metal falls pressured by the strength of the dollar and expectations that interest rates will remain high for longer.

title: Oil falls due to progress in negotiations between the United States and Iran, while Eni and Mercuria formalize an alliance
summary: Crude oil prices ease on expectations of an increase in Iranian supply, as major energy traders partner up.

title: Ibex 35 starts July with moderate declines, with Indra as a bullish stock and Acciona Energía weighing on the index
summary: The Spanish stock market trades lower in the first session of the semester, with attention on the Fed and the evolution of inflation in Europe.

title: Getty Images cancels $3.7 billion merger with Shutterstock after British regulator veto
summary: The UK competition authority considers that the operation would have significantly reduced competition in the stock image market.

title: Google ordered to pay $2 billion to Klarna for antitrust damages
summary: A US court finds the tech giant liable for restrictive practices that harmed the Swedish online payments company.

title: Lime, the electric scooter service backed by Uber, debuts on the stock exchange raising $167 million
summary: The urban micromobility company goes public at a time of boom in sustainable transportation in cities around the world.

title: US nuclear regulator approves Oklo reactor's safety analysis, a milestone towards operating license
summary: The advanced nuclear energy startup, backed by Sam Altman, passes a critical step to build its first reactor in the country.
